title: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26
display_title: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26
wikitext:
[]: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Nov 23, 2010 5:07 pm I always wondered about those cue cards and who was actually analyzing the characters in the end of EoTV. Instrumentiality is a strange beast but the cue cards came off as the most outlandish in my case, what exactly the cue card thing about? Who was analyzing the characters?

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Nov 23, 2010 5:23 pm GAP wrote: I always wondered about those cue cards and who was actually analyzing the characters in the end of EoTV. Instrumentiality is a strange beast but the cue cards came off as the most outlandish in my case, what exactly the cue card thing about? Who was analyzing the characters?

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 23, 2010 5:29 pm The general consciousness, I suppose. skikes wrote: I've thought about this myself. It just seems kind of a mess. If my memory serves me right I believe Rei's is labelled "In the case of Rei Ayanami: part one" but she never gets a part two?

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 23, 2010 5:41 pm GAP wrote: Who was analyzing the characters?

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Nov 23, 2010 5:44 pm esselfortium wrote: The general consciousness, I suppose. Wasn't it "Part one: In the case of Rei Ayanami"?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 23, 2010 6:03 pm From the LTP , the relevant lines are Ikari Shinji. In his case, Soryuu Asuka Langley. In her case, The third character Ayanami Rei, in her case, CASE 1 (PART 1 In the case of Katsuragi Misato CASE 2 In the case of Soryuu Asuka Langley. Part 1 in episode 25 and Case 3: In the case of Ikari Shinji. in episode 26

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Wed Nov 24, 2010 10:13 pm GAP wrote: I always wondered about those cue cards and who was actually analyzing the characters in the end of EoTV. Instrumentiality is a strange beast but the cue cards came off as the most outlandish in my case, what exactly the cue card thing about? Who was analyzing the characters?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 10:17 pm I figured it to be the flash Rei from One More Final - "the existence that gazes upon man" and all that. But also, in a way, it's identified directly with Hideaki Anno - and of course we, the audience of EoE and EoTV alike, are also identified with that flash Rei, watching Shinji in order to understand him but unable to affect him.

[]: Re: The cue cards in Episode 25 and 26             Quote Post by Chrad » Fri Nov 26, 2010 7:30 am GAP wrote: I always wondered about those cue cards and who was actually analyzing the characters in the end of EoTV. Instrumentiality is a strange beast but the cue cards came off as the most outlandish in my case, what exactly the cue card thing about? Who was analyzing the characters?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 6:02 pm beaverteeth92 wrote: It seems like the cue cards are the inner voices of the characters. Like each character is questioning him/herself these questions and is having a philosophical dilemma or self-analysis.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Dec 20, 2010 7:52 am Azathoth wrote: I figured it to be the flash Rei from One More Final - "the existence that gazes upon man" and all that. But also, in a way, it's identified directly with Hideaki Anno - and of course we, the audience of EoE and EoTV alike, are also identified with that flash Rei, watching Shinji in order to understand him but unable to affect him.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Dec 20, 2010 5:17 pm I honestly think that things like this are open to interpretation (like almost everything in NGE), so it seems like as long as it makes sense, it's a valid estimate.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Dec 20, 2010 5:20 pm Azathoth wrote: I figured it to be the flash Rei from One More Final - "the existence that gazes upon man" and all that. But also, in a way, it's identified directly with Hideaki Anno - and of course we, the audience of EoE and EoTV alike, are also identified with that flash Rei, watching Shinji in order to understand him but unable to affect him.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Dec 20, 2010 5:25 pm Xard wrote: Gosh I hate myself for forgetting this but where did "the existence that gazes upon man" come from
